checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary validation at ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH supporting ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH

reachability.notes: Attacker can invoke precompile via on-chain transaction with arbitrary calldata length.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Trace data flow from boundary ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH through node ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH.
2. Property context: The secp256r1 precompile input must be exactly 160 bytes. Inputs of any other length must cause the precompile to return empty output without reverting.
3. Verify that ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.
executable_checks: Trace data flow from ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH to ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH
Verify ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH handles edge cases from boundary input
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W0-EIP7951-PRECOND-001. Supporting node for boundary ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary validation at ACTION-RETRIEVE-COLUMN-SIDECARS supporting EDGE-P2P-SUPPLY-SIDECARS

reachability.notes: P2P peers are untrusted; all sidecar data must be validated.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Trace data flow from boundary EDGE-P2P-SUPPLY-SIDECARS through node ACTION-RETRIEVE-COLUMN-SIDECARS.
2. Property context: Column sidecars fetched from P2P peers for data availability must undergo the same validation as gossip-received sidecars: format verification followed by KZG proof verification.
3. Verify that ACTION-RETRIEVE-COLUMN-SIDECARS correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.
executable_checks: Trace data flow from EDGE-P2P-SUPPLY-SIDECARS to ACTION-RETRIEVE-COLUMN-SIDECARS
Verify ACTION-RETRIEVE-COLUMN-SIDECARS handles edge cases from boundary input
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W0-FULUFC-PRECOND-008. Supporting node for boundary EDGE-P2P-SUPPLY-SIDECARS.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition on ACTION-ANNOUNCE-TO-PEERS: After validation passes, a provider must announce with all-ones cell_m

reachability.notes: If a sampler announces all-ones cell_mask without having all data, downstream requests will fail.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Post-condition: After validation passes, a provider must announce with all-ones cell_mask, and a sampler must propagate based on its actually available data.
2. Execute the operation with various valid inputs.
3. After each execution, verify:
   a. All expected side-effects occurred correctly
   b. No unexpected state changes occurred
   c. Return values match the post-condition specification
4. Test with boundary inputs that are valid but extreme.
5. Check partial execution/failure scenarios.
6. Severity justification: False all-ones announcements from samplers would cause widespread cell request failures, affecting >5% of blob propagation.
executable_checks: Execute operation on ACTION-ANNOUNCE-TO-PEERS and verify all post-condition guarantees
Check for unexpected side-effects after operation completion
Test partial failure scenarios for post-condition violations
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W1-EIP8070-POSTCOND-001. Type: Post-condition. Primary element: ACTION-ANNOUNCE-TO-PEERS.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary validation at ACTION-VERIFY-STRUCTURAL-VALIDITY supporting EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE

reachability.notes: First validation barrier against malformed gossip data

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Trace data flow from boundary EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE through node ACTION-VERIFY-STRUCTURAL-VALIDITY.
2. Property context: Before accepting a data_column_sidecar from gossip (EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE boundary), the node MUST run verify_data_column_sidecar to check: sidecar.index < NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS, len(kzg_commitments) > 0, l
3. Verify that ACTION-VERIFY-STRUCTURAL-VALIDITY correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.
executable_checks: Trace data flow from EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE to ACTION-VERIFY-STRUCTURAL-VALIDITY
Verify ACTION-VERIFY-STRUCTURAL-VALIDITY handles edge cases from boundary input
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-PRECOND-01. Supporting node for boundary EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION: The Fulu fork transition must only occur at an epoch boundary where sl

reachability.notes: If the fork condition check is incorrect, nodes could fork at different epochs, causing a network split. This is an internal logic bug, not directly attacker-triggered.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: The Fulu fork transition must only occur at an epoch boundary where slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH == 0 AND compute_epoch_at_slot(slot) == FULU_FORK_EPOCH (411392).
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Incorrect fork activation epoch would split the entire network into incompatible chains.
executable_checks: Create state transition sequence targeting ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION invariant violation
Test boundary values for state transitions affecting ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION
Verify invariant holds under concurrent modifications
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W3-FULU-FORK-TRANSITION-INV-001. Type: Invariant. Primary element: ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-CHECK-BLOB-COMMITMENTS-LIMIT: Blocks received from P2P peers must have their blob_kzg_commitments co

reachability.notes: Using a hardcoded limit instead of the dynamic blob schedule would cause rejection of valid blocks after blob parameter updates.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: Blocks received from P2P peers must have their blob_kzg_commitments count validated against the dynamic limit from get_blob_parameters for the block's epoch, not a hardcoded constant.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Hardcoded blob limit would cause block rejection divergence after blob schedule changes.
executable_checks: Attempt precondition bypass on ACTION-CHECK-BLOB-COMMITMENTS-LIMIT with crafted invalid inputs
Test edge-case values (zero, max, overflow) against precondition
Verify rejection behavior is consistent across all code paths
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W3-FULU-EXEC-PAYLOAD-PRE-004. Type: Pre-condition. Primary element: ACTION-CHECK-BLOB-COMMITMENTS-LIMIT.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary validation at ACTION-PARSE-INPUT-LENGTHS supporting EDGE-CALL-ARRIVES

reachability.notes: Any EOA or contract can call ModExp with arbitrary calldata via on-chain transaction. Malformed input handling differences between clients could cause consensus splits.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Trace data flow from boundary EDGE-CALL-ARRIVES through node ACTION-PARSE-INPUT-LENGTHS.
2. Property context: ModExp precompile calldata must contain at least 96 bytes for the three length fields (base_length, modulus_length, exponent_length). Short calldata must be zero-padded, not rejected.
3. Verify that ACTION-PARSE-INPUT-LENGTHS correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.
executable_checks: Trace data flow from EDGE-CALL-ARRIVES to ACTION-PARSE-INPUT-LENGTHS
Verify ACTION-PARSE-INPUT-LENGTHS handles edge cases from boundary input
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W4-EIP7883-PRECOND-001. Supporting node for boundary EDGE-CALL-ARRIVES.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-CHECK-GAS-LIMIT: Gas limit validation MUST be based on the declared gasLimit field, not

reachability.notes: If some clients check actual usage vs. declared limit differently, consensus divergence occurs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: Gas limit validation MUST be based on the declared gasLimit field, not actual gas usage; a transaction with gasLimit > 16,777,216 is invalid regardless of how much gas it would actually consume.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Divergent interpretation of the gas cap check between clients would cause a consensus split.
executable_checks: Attempt precondition bypass on ACTION-CHECK-GAS-LIMIT with crafted invalid inputs
Test edge-case values (zero, max, overflow) against precondition
Verify rejection behavior is consistent across all code paths
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W5-EIP7825-TX-AMB-001. Type: Pre-condition. Primary element: ACTION-CHECK-GAS-LIMIT.

reachability.notes: Untrusted P2P peer can send mismatched array lengths. Missing check could cause out-of-bounds access.

3. Verify that STATE-CELLS-RECEIVED-FOR-VERIFY correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Effective Balance Manipulation Prevention in Proposer Selection

reachability.notes: Attacker may try to manipulate effective balance to influence proposer selection.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify that the proposer lookahead uses effective balances frozen at the epoch boundary. 2. Attempt to change a validator's effective balance within the lookahead window and verify the proposer assignment doesn't change. 3. Verify that the seed used for proposer selection is deterministic and based on RANDAO at the correct epoch. 4. Test that the lookahead is recomputed correctly when epoch transitions occur.
executable_checks: Simulate effective balance change mid-epoch and verify no lookahead change
Verify RANDAO seed usage in proposer selection
Cross-client comparison of proposer assignments
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7917-PRECOND-004. Pre-condition property, Formal Verification mindset. EIP-7917 prevents effective balance manipulation from affecting the already-computed lookahead.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for eth/69 ForkID Validation During Handshake

reachability.notes: Malicious peer can send crafted ForkID in eth/69 Status message.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Connect to a node using eth/69 and send a Status message with an invalid ForkID (wrong hash or wrong next fork). 2. Verify the node disconnects the peer. 3. Test with ForkID from a pre-Fusaka chain. 4. Test with ForkID that has correct hash but wrong next value. 5. Test with ForkID from a different network (different genesis). 6. Verify that the dropped pre-merge fields (totalDifficulty) don't cause parsing errors.
executable_checks: Send eth/69 Status with invalid ForkID variants
Verify disconnection for incompatible ForkIDs
Test eth/69 without totalDifficulty field
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-EIP7642-HANDSHAKE-PRECOND-03. Boundary edge EDGE-P2P-ETH69-HANDSHAKE. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. EIP-7642 drops pre-merge fields from eth/69; ForkID validation must still work correctly.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition: get_beacon_proposer_index Uses Pre-computed Lookahead

reachability.notes: get_beacon_proposer_index must use the pre-computed proposer_lookahead, not recompute.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Initialize a Fulu state with proposer_lookahead populated. 2. Call get_beacon_proposer_index and verify it returns the value from proposer_lookahead[slot % len(proposer_lookahead)]. 3. Modify the state (change balances, activate/exit validators) and verify the returned proposer doesn't change within the same lookahead window. 4. Verify the function does NOT fall back to compute_proposer_index.
executable_checks: Call get_beacon_proposer_index and compare with lookahead array
Modify state and verify no lookahead change within window
Verify no fallback to compute_proposer_index
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-BEACON-LOOKAHEAD-POST-001. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. EIP-7917 requires using pre-computed values.

checklist_item.title: Falsify ModExp Multiplication Complexity at 32-Byte Boundary

reachability.notes: Attacker controls base_length and modulus_length. Boundary at 32 bytes is critical.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Call ModExp precompile with max(base_length, modulus_length) = 32. Verify complexity = 16. 2. Call with max = 33. Verify complexity = 2 * ceil(33/8)^2 = 2 * 5^2 = 50. 3. Call with max = 31. Verify complexity = 16 (minimum). 4. Test with max = 0, 1, 8, 16, 32, 33, 64, 128, 1024. 5. Cross-client comparison of gas consumed for each case. 6. Verify the new EIP-7883 formula is used post-Fusaka (not the old formula).
executable_checks: Call ModExp with max lengths at 32/33 boundary
Cross-client gas consumption comparison
Verify EIP-7883 formula activation at Fusaka
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4-EIP7883-POSTCOND-001. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. EIP-7883 changes ModExp gas cost. The 32-byte boundary is a discontinuity point.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition: Valid Block Input for DataColumnSidecar Construction

reachability.notes: If EL provides invalid blob data via Engine API, constructed sidecars would be invalid.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Simulate an EL returning a BlobsBundle via Engine API with invalid cell KZG proofs. 2. Verify the CL validates the proofs before constructing DataColumnSidecars. 3. If the CL does NOT validate, verify that invalid sidecars propagated to the network are caught by other nodes. 4. Test with: mismatched commitment counts, invalid proof lengths, proofs for wrong blobs.
executable_checks: Provide invalid BlobsBundle via Engine API mock
Verify CL validation before sidecar construction
Test propagation impact of unvalidated EL data
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-VAL-SIDECAR-PRECOND-001. Internal property, Formal Verification mindset. Engine API is semi-trusted; CL should still validate blob data from EL.

checklist_item.title: Violate gas cost invariant by testing all input patterns for secp256r1 precompile
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Invoke secp256r1 precompile with diverse input patterns including: minimal inputs, maximal inputs, edge cases, valid/invalid curve points, zero values, maximum field values. 2. Instrument gas metering to capture exact gas consumption per invocation. 3. Compare gas costs across all test cases. 4. Identify any deviation from fixed cost of 3450 gas. 5. Attempt state transitions that might affect gas calculation (e.g., cold vs warm access, different block contexts). 6. Document any conditions causing variable gas consumption.
executable_checks: Invoke with zero public key point - measure gas = 3450
Invoke with valid curve point - measure gas = 3450
Invoke with invalid curve point - measure gas = 3450
Invoke with maximum field values - measure gas = 3450
Invoke with minimal non-zero values - measure gas = 3450
Invoke in cold state vs warm state - both must cost 3450 gas
Invoke multiple times in same transaction - each must cost 3450 gas
Compare gas cost across different signature messages - all must be 3450
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W0-EIP7951-INV-005 covering secp256r1 precompile gas cost invariant

checklist_item.title: Violate fork atomicity invariant through concurrent state transitions
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Start node in STATE-PRE-FORK. 2. Trigger fork activation at specific block height. 3. During ACTION-APPLY-FORK-RULES execution, submit concurrent blocks for validation. 4. Monitor which validation rules are applied (pre-fork vs post-fork). 5. Check if any block is validated with mixed rules. 6. Verify STATE-FUSAKA-ACTIVE is reached atomically without intermediate states. 7. Test race conditions by pausing/resuming threads during fork transition. 8. Verify rollback mechanisms if fork activation fails mid-transition.
executable_checks: Submit block for validation during fork activation and verify single ruleset applied
Check STATE-PRE-FORK and STATE-FUSAKA-ACTIVE are mutually exclusive
Verify no blocks processed during transition window with mixed validation
Test concurrent ACTION-APPLY-FORK-RULES invocations and verify mutual exclusion
Monitor state machine for intermediate states between pre/post fork
Verify database transactions are atomic during fork activation
Check validator state is consistent before and after transition
Simulate crash during fork activation and verify clean recovery to one state
Verify all subsystems (validator, p2p, storage) transition simultaneously
Test that rollback to pre-fork state is complete if activation fails
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W1-EIP7607-INV-001 covering nodes=[STATE-PRE-FORK, ACTION-APPLY-FORK-RULES, STATE-FUSAKA-ACTIVE]

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition: Sidecar not stored after passing all validation rules
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft a valid data_column_sidecar that passes all 13 validation rules. 2. Submit via gossip network. 3. Monitor validation pipeline to confirm all rules pass and STATE-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR-ACCEPTED is reached. 4. Immediately query local DA storage for the sidecar by (slot, column_index). 5. Verify sidecar is stored and retrievable. 6. Issue DataColumnSidecarsByRange or DataColumnSidecarsByRoot request for the sidecar and verify it is served. 7. Test storage failure scenarios: disk full, DB errors, race conditions.
executable_checks: Submit valid data_column_sidecar and verify it reaches STATE-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR-ACCEPTED
Query local DA storage for accepted sidecar by (slot, column_index) and verify presence
Issue DataColumnSidecarsByRange request covering accepted sidecar and verify it is served
Issue DataColumnSidecarsByRoot request for accepted sidecar and verify it is served
Simulate storage write failure and verify sidecar is not marked as ACCEPTED
Test race condition: submit same sidecar twice and verify single storage entry
Verify sidecar availability immediately after acceptance with zero delay
Check that all 13 validation rules passed before storage is attempted
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-POSTCOND-01 covering node STATE-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR-ACCEPTED and edge EDGE-ALL-VALID

checklist_item.title: Verify Local Node Correctly Computes and Advertises Fork Digest
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Start local node with known genesis_validators_root and FULU_FORK_VERSION. 2. Capture fork digest advertised via ENR and STATUS messages. 3. Independently compute expected fork digest. 4. Compare advertised vs. expected digest. 5. Test across different network configurations. 6. Verify fork digest updates correctly at fork boundary. 7. Check ENR updates propagate fork digest change to discovery layer.
executable_checks: Query local node ENR, extract fork digest field, verify matches compute_fork_digest(FULU_FORK_VERSION, genesis_validators_root)
Intercept outbound STATUS message to peer, verify fork_digest field contains correct value
Start node with custom genesis_validators_root, confirm advertised fork digest incorporates custom root
Advance node across Electra->Fulu fork boundary, verify advertised fork digest updates
Test with multiple network configs, verify each advertises correct fork digest
Verify fork digest computation uses domain separation
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W3-FULU-FORK-DIGEST-PRE-001 covering ACTION-ADVERTISE-FORK-DIGEST, STATE-PEER-CONNECTED, STATE-PEER-REJECTED

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for P2P_to_BlockValidation: Reject blocks with inconsistent excess_blob_gas
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify P2P block ingestion entry point. 2. Craft malicious block with excess_blob_gas inconsistent with parent and blob schedule. 3. Submit block via P2P interface. 4. Verify block is rejected BEFORE entering internal validation pipeline. 5. Confirm rejection reason indicates excess_blob_gas validation failure. 6. Verify no state mutation occurred.
executable_checks: Submit block via P2P with excess_blob_gas = 0 when parent requires non-zero value: verify immediate rejection
Submit block with excess_blob_gas computed using wrong blob_schedule.target: verify rejection at boundary
Submit block with excess_blob_gas exceeding maximum possible value: verify overflow detection
Verify excess_blob_gas validation occurs BEFORE signature verification, BEFORE execution
Confirm rejection does not propagate invalid block to peers
Test with blob schedule fork transition boundary: verify correct schedule used for validation
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W4-EIP7892-PRECOND-006 covering trust boundary P2P_to_BlockValidation

checklist_item.title: Falsify Determinism: Force Divergent proposer_lookahead Arrays via State Transitions
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify all state inputs to proposer_lookahead computation. 2. Create two identical initial states. 3. Apply parallel state transitions with identical inputs. 4. Execute proposer_lookahead computation independently. 5. Compare resulting arrays element-by-element. 6. Inject non-deterministic sources: unsorted iterators, floating-point ops, race conditions, uninitialized memory.
executable_checks: Run proposer_lookahead computation twice with identical epoch boundary state, compare arrays for exact equality
Inject concurrent state modifications during lookahead computation to test for race conditions
Replace validator set iteration with reverse order to test for ordering dependencies
Test with different memory allocation patterns to detect uninitialized memory reads
Fuzz state transition paths leading to lookahead recomputation with identical inputs
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W6-FULU-FORK-ST-INV-001 covering proposer_lookahead_computation

checklist_item.title: Falsify Security Invariance: Identify Contracts with Changed Security Properties Under 60M Gas Limit
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Survey existing smart contracts relying on block gas limit for security. 2. Simulate execution under 36M and 60M limits. 3. Identify contracts where increased limit enables new attack vectors. 4. Test specific patterns: withdrawal contracts, auction mechanisms, governance systems. 5. Verify if security assumptions break solely due to gas limit increase.
executable_checks: Identify contracts using block.gaslimit in security-critical logic and simulate attacks with 60M limit
Test reentrancy guards relying on gas limits to prevent deep call stacks with 60M gas blocks
Simulate griefing attacks on withdrawal queues that were infeasible at 36M but possible at 60M
Analyze MEV searcher strategies that become viable only with 60M gas blocks
Test auction contracts with gas-limited finality assumptions under 60M blocks
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W7-EIP7935-ASSUM-INV-001 covering smart_contract_security_model

checklist_item.title: Falsify checkpoint update post-condition after block acceptance

reachability.notes: Incorrect checkpoint updates could stall finality.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Process a block whose post-state has a new justified checkpoint higher than the current store justified checkpoint. 2. Verify update_checkpoints is called with the post-state's current_justified_checkpoint and finalized_checkpoint. 3. Attempt to falsify: process a block and verify that store.justified_checkpoint is NOT updated when it should be (bug: missing call). 4. Check edge case: block whose post-state justified checkpoint equals the store's current one (no-op expected).
executable_checks: State test: after block with new justified checkpoint, assert store.justified_checkpoint updated
State test: after block with same justified checkpoint, assert store.justified_checkpoint unchanged
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-POSTCOND-002. Formal Verification mindset: attempting to find a state transition where checkpoints are not updated when they should be.

checklist_item.title: Falsify fork activation timestamp comparison correctness

reachability.notes: Premature fork activation due to timestamp comparison bug would cause consensus split.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify that fork rules are NOT applied when block.timestamp < activation_timestamp. 2. Verify fork rules ARE applied when block.timestamp == activation_timestamp (boundary). 3. Verify fork rules ARE applied when block.timestamp > activation_timestamp. 4. Test with timestamp = activation_timestamp - 1, activation_timestamp, activation_timestamp + 1. 5. Cross-client comparison of activation logic.
executable_checks: Test: block at timestamp activation-1, assert pre-fork rules
Test: block at timestamp activation, assert post-fork rules
Test: block at timestamp activation+1, assert post-fork rules
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP7607-PRECOND-001. Pre-condition: >= comparison required.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-EL-TRUST: engine_getBlobsV2 trust assumption

attacker_controlled: Blob data if EL is compromised
validation_layers: NONE - verification explicitly skipped per AMB-FULU-P2P-05
reachability.notes: CRITICAL: ASSUM-FULU-P2P-01 and AMB-FULU-P2P-05 - EL is trusted, no KZG verification on local blobs

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Simulate a compromised EL that returns corrupted blob data via engine_getBlobsV2. 2. Verify whether the CL converts these corrupted blobs into data columns WITHOUT KZG verification. 3. If yes, verify these corrupted columns are gossiped to the network. 4. Assess impact: how many nodes would accept the corrupted columns before KZG verification catches them at gossip validation on receiving peers. 5. Check: does the anti-equivocation cache cover locally-imported columns?
executable_checks: Integration test: mock EL returning corrupted blobs, verify CL behavior
Verify: CL skips KZG for local blobs (confirm the trust assumption)
Verify: anti-equivocation cache includes locally-imported data columns
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-BLOB-PUB-PRECOND-01. CRITICAL trust boundary. Boundary edge: EDGE-ENGINE-API-GET-BLOBS-V2. Related: AMB-FULU-P2P-04 (SHOULD→MUST upgrade recommended).

checklist_item.title: Falsify BPO fork activation timestamp boundary on EL

reachability.notes: Off-by-one in timestamp comparison at fork boundary could cause the first post-fork block to use wrong parameters.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Set bpoTime to a known timestamp T. 2. Submit block with timestamp T-1: verify pre-BPO parameters used. 3. Submit block with timestamp T: verify post-BPO parameters used (>= comparison). 4. Submit block with timestamp T+1: verify post-BPO parameters used. 5. Cross-client: verify all EL implementations use >= (not >) for activation comparison.
executable_checks: Test: block.timestamp == bpoTime - 1, assert pre-fork blob params
Test: block.timestamp == bpoTime, assert post-fork blob params
Cross-client: verify >= comparison in all EL implementations
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4-EIP7892-PRECOND-003. Pre-condition: >= comparison at exact activation timestamp.

checklist_item.title: Falsify premature Fulu fork activation guard

reachability.notes: Premature fork activation would split the network.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Process slots at epoch FULU_FORK_EPOCH - 1. Verify state remains in Electra format. 2. Process a slot at epoch FULU_FORK_EPOCH. Verify fork upgrade triggers. 3. Attempt to falsify: check if any code path can trigger Fulu upgrade before FULU_FORK_EPOCH. 4. Test: check integer overflow scenarios if FULU_FORK_EPOCH is set to MAX_UINT64 (disabled).
executable_checks: Test: epoch = FULU_FORK_EPOCH - 1, assert Electra state format
Test: epoch = FULU_FORK_EPOCH, assert Fulu state format
Test: FULU_FORK_EPOCH = FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH, assert fork never triggers
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-FORK-ST-INV-002. Invariant: no Fulu upgrade outside FULU_FORK_EPOCH.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-UNTRUSTED-INPUT: byte length validation for PeerDAS data

reachability.notes: Primary trust boundary for PeerDAS. Any malicious peer can send arbitrary bytes.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send raw bytes that are too short for a commitment (< BYTES_PER_COMMITMENT). Verify rejection. 2. Send bytes too long for a commitment. Verify rejection. 3. Repeat for proofs (BYTES_PER_PROOF) and cells (BYTES_PER_CELL). 4. Send completely empty payload. Verify rejection. 5. Verify the length check occurs BEFORE any deserialization attempt (no partial parsing).
executable_checks: P2P test: commitment with BYTES_PER_COMMITMENT - 1 bytes, assert rejection
P2P test: proof with BYTES_PER_PROOF + 1 bytes, assert rejection
P2P test: cell with wrong length, assert rejection
Verify: length check is first validation step (before deserialization)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-SECBND-PRECOND-001. Boundary edge: EDGE-UNTRUSTED-INPUT. First line of defense for PeerDAS.

checklist_item.title: Falsify minimum custody group assignment guarantee (CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT >= 4)
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Generate a large set of random node_ids (at least 10,000). 2. For each node_id, call get_custody_groups(node_id) or equivalent. 3. Verify len(result) >= CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT (4) for every node_id. 4. Edge cases: (a) node_id = 0, (b) node_id = 2^256 - 1, (c) node_id values that produce hash collisions in the custody group computation. 5. Verify the total number of unique groups across all node_ids covers all NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS groups.
executable_checks: Enumerate 10,000 random node_ids, verify min custody group count >= 4
Test node_id = 0 and node_id = max_uint256
Verify union of all custody groups covers full group range
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-INV-002. Invariant falsification. Under-assigned custody groups reduce data availability coverage.

checklist_item.title: Falsify parent-seen check for data column sidecar gossip validation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a valid data_column_sidecar referencing a parent_root that the target node has NOT seen. 2. Send it via gossip. 3. Verify the node IGNOREs the sidecar (does not process it, does not store it). 4. Then send the actual parent block, followed by the sidecar again. 5. Verify the sidecar is now accepted. 6. Edge case: send sidecar referencing a parent from an alternate fork that was later pruned.
executable_checks: Send sidecar with unknown parent_root, verify IGNORE
Send parent block then re-send sidecar, verify acceptance
Verify no state changes from ignored sidecars
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-PRECOND-06. Pre-condition falsification. Parent check prevents orphan processing.

checklist_item.title: Falsify EIP-7918 constant values (BLOB_BASE_COST and GAS_PER_BLOB)
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. In each EL client implementation, locate the BLOB_BASE_COST constant definition. 2. Verify BLOB_BASE_COST == 8192 (2^13). 3. Locate GAS_PER_BLOB constant. Verify GAS_PER_BLOB == 131072 (2^17). 4. Compute the ratio: BLOB_BASE_COST / GAS_PER_BLOB. Verify it equals 1/16. 5. Cross-check across all EL client implementations (Geth, Nethermind, Besu, Reth, Erigon). 6. Verify no integer overflow in reserve price calculation: BLOB_BASE_COST * base_fee_per_gas (could overflow uint64 at high base fees).
executable_checks: Verify BLOB_BASE_COST == 8192 in each client
Verify GAS_PER_BLOB == 131072 in each client
Check for integer overflow in BLOB_BASE_COST * max_possible_base_fee_per_gas
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-EIP7918-RESERVE-INV-003. Invariant falsification. Wrong constants would affect every block's fee calculation, causing immediate consensus split.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for KZG verification before erasure code reconstruction
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Provide sufficient columns (>= 50%) for reconstruction, but include one column with invalid data (modified cell content but original KZG proof). 2. Verify the individual KZG verification catches the invalid column BEFORE reconstruction begins. 3. Test: provide a column with a valid KZG proof for different data (proof from column X applied to column Y). 4. Test: provide a column with all-zero proof. 5. Verify reconstruction does NOT proceed with any unverified column. 6. Verify the corrupted reconstruction result (if verification is skipped) would propagate incorrect blob data.
executable_checks: Supply column with mismatched data/proof, verify rejection before reconstruction
Supply column with proof from different column, verify rejection
Verify reconstruction only uses KZG-verified columns
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-RECON-PRECOND-001. Boundary property. Trust boundary: P2P peer provides columns for reconstruction. Corrupted reconstruction could propagate incorrect blob data to the network.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for full DataColumnSidecar validation on P2P gossip
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send a crafted DataColumnSidecar via P2P pubsub with: (a) invalid KZG proof, (b) invalid Merkle inclusion proof, (c) column_index >= NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS, (d) slot in the far future, (e) invalid proposer signature. 2. For each case, verify the sidecar is REJECTED. 3. Test bypass: valid KZG proof but invalid Merkle proof (and vice versa). 4. Test bypass: all proofs valid but for a different block (replay attack). 5. Verify the validation order prevents early exit before all checks complete. 6. Monitor for any crash, panic, or undefined behavior on malformed input.
executable_checks: Send sidecar with invalid KZG proof, verify rejection
Send sidecar with invalid Merkle proof but valid KZG, verify rejection
Send sidecar with out-of-range column_index, verify rejection without crash
Send sidecar with invalid proposer signature, verify rejection
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-VAL-TRUST-PRECOND-001. Boundary property. Trust boundary: unauthenticated P2P peers send arbitrary crafted sidecars. This is the PRIMARY attack surface for PeerDAS. Missing validation could lead to state corruption or crashes across all vulnerable nodes.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-P2P-BLOCK-TO-FORKCHOICE: reject blocks at or before finalized slot
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Set up a test node that has finalized at epoch E (slot S_fin = compute_start_slot_at_epoch(E)). 2. Craft a SignedBeaconBlock with slot == S_fin and a valid parent root. Send via P2P gossip. 3. Verify the block is REJECTED by on_block. 4. Craft a block with slot == S_fin - 1. Send via P2P. Verify REJECTED. 5. Craft a block with slot == S_fin + 1 with valid parent. Verify it proceeds past this check. 6. Check that the rejection fires before any state transition or DB writes occur.
executable_checks: Assert block with slot == finalized_slot is rejected with appropriate error
Assert block with slot < finalized_slot is rejected
Assert block with slot == finalized_slot + 1 passes this specific check
Assert no state mutation occurs for rejected blocks
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-PRECOND-003. Boundary property. Trust model edge EDGE-P2P-BLOCK-TO-FORKCHOICE requires full validation pipeline. Attacker replays old finalized blocks via P2P. Accepting blocks at or before finalized slot could revert finality.

checklist_item.title: Falsify BPO fork timestamp ordering: verify BPO1 < BPO2 < BPO3 > Fusaka activation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Read all BPO fork timestamps from the chain config. 2. Verify strict ordering: FUSAKA_TIMESTAMP < BPO1_TIMESTAMP < BPO2_TIMESTAMP < BPO3_TIMESTAMP. 3. Test what happens if BPO timestamps are equal (BPO1 == BPO2): does the client handle this? 4. Test with reversed ordering and verify the client either rejects the config or handles it correctly. 5. Cross-check multiple client implementations for identical timestamp parsing.
executable_checks: Assert BPO1_TIMESTAMP > FUSAKA_TIMESTAMP
Assert BPO2_TIMESTAMP > BPO1_TIMESTAMP
Assert BPO3_TIMESTAMP > BPO2_TIMESTAMP
Assert client rejects or handles equal BPO timestamps
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP7607-INV-002. Invariant type. Related ambiguity AMB-EIP7607-01. Misordered BPO activation could cause consensus split between nodes interpreting different blob parameters.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-REQRESP-DATA-COLUMNS-BY-RANGE: enforce serve range limits
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Compute expected data_column_serve_range = [max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH), current_epoch]. 2. Request columns for a slot BEFORE the serve range. Verify ResourceUnavailable (error 3). 3. Request columns for a slot AFTER current epoch. Verify rejection. 4. Request columns at exact boundary of serve range. Verify correct serving. 5. Request with count exceeding MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS. Verify truncation or rejection. 6. Test immediately after FULU_FORK_EPOCH when serve range is minimal.
executable_checks: Assert requests before serve range return ResourceUnavailable
Assert requests after current epoch return error
Assert boundary slots are served correctly
Assert count > MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS is handled
Assert serve range correctly uses max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-REQRESP-PRECOND-02. Boundary property. Trust model edge EDGE-REQRESP-DATA-COLUMNS-BY-RANGE: range must be checked, response limited, ordering enforced.

checklist_item.title: Falsify monotonicity of excess_blob_gas in reserve mode
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Simulate a sequence of blocks in reserve mode with varying blob_gas_used values. 2. For each block, verify excess_blob_gas[n+1] >= excess_blob_gas[n] (monotonically non-decreasing). 3. Test edge case: blob_gas_used = 0 in reserve mode. The formula should still add (since it only adds in reserve mode). 4. Test transition from reserve to normal mode: verify excess_blob_gas may now decrease. 5. Verify the formula: excess = parent.excess + parent.blob_gas_used * BLOB_BASE_COST / parent.base_fee always produces non-negative addition.
executable_checks: Assert excess_blob_gas never decreases in reserve mode across block sequences
Assert excess_blob_gas may decrease in normal mode when blob_gas_used < target
Assert formula correctness for blob_gas_used = 0 in reserve mode
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-EIP7918-EXCESS-INV-002. Invariant type. Violations of monotonicity in reserve mode indicate implementation bugs that would cause consensus divergence.

checklist_item.title: Verify MODEXP consumes all gas and returns error when input exceeds 1024 bytes
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Call MODEXP with base_length = 1025 (just over limit). Measure gas consumed. Verify ALL gas is consumed. 2. Repeat for exp_length = 1025, mod_length = 1025. 3. Verify the return value is empty/error (not a valid result). 4. Test boundary: exactly 1024 bytes → must succeed normally with standard gas cost. 5. Test with all three lengths at 1024 → must succeed. 6. Test with one at 1025 and others at 0 → must consume all gas. 7. Cross-check gas consumption behavior across all EL clients for IDENTICAL behavior.
executable_checks: Assert input with any length > 1024 consumes ALL provided gas
Assert input with all lengths <= 1024 executes normally
Assert return value is empty/error for over-limit inputs
Assert boundary 1024 works, 1025 fails
Assert identical gas behavior across all EL clients
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7823-VAL-POSTCOND-001. Post-condition type. Related ambiguity AMB-EIP7823-01. Different gas consumption across clients on violation causes consensus split, crashing the entire network.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-BLOCK-GOSSIP-CL: reject oversized blocks at gossip layer
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft a beacon block that exceeds 10 MiB in serialized (SSZ) form. 2. Send via gossip. Verify the receiving node rejects it before full deserialization. 3. Verify the check is at the gossip layer (libp2p message size), not at block processing. 4. Test exactly at 10 MiB boundary: 10 MiB should pass, 10 MiB + 1 byte should fail. 5. Verify rejected oversized messages are NOT forwarded. 6. Verify the 10 MiB limit accounts for SSZ encoding, not just payload.
executable_checks: Assert block > 10 MiB is rejected at gossip layer
Assert block <= 10 MiB is accepted for further processing
Assert rejection happens before full deserialization
Assert rejected messages not forwarded to peers
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-EIP7935-SECBND-POSTCOND-001. Boundary property. Trust model edge EDGE-BLOCK-GOSSIP-CL: block size validated against 10 MiB limit. Failure enables gossip network flooding.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-FINALIZED-SLOT-OK-TO-ANCESTRY: validate pre-condition at entry point

reachability.notes: Attacker crafts blocks that don't descend from finalized checkpoint to create alternative chains.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-FINALIZED-SLOT-OK-TO-ANCESTRY where untrusted data enters via P2P. 2. Review input validation: A block must descend from the finalized checkpoint block: get_checkpoint_block must match store.finalized_checkpoint.root. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via P2P and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting EDGE-FINALIZED-SLOT-OK-TO-ANCESTRY boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-PRECOND-004. Mindset: Boundary Guard. Property: A block must descend from the finalized checkpoint block: get_checkpoint_block must match store.finalized_checkpoint.root.. Graph: nodes=['ACTION-CHECK-FINALIZED-DESCENDANT'], edges=['EDGE-FINALIZED-SLOT-OK-TO-ANCESTRY', 'EDGE-ANCESTRY-FAIL'].

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at STATE-COLUMN-SIDECAR-RECEIVED

reachability.notes: Attacker can send a DataColumnSidecar with an out-of-bounds column index via gossip or Req/Resp to crash or confuse the node.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-RECEIVE-SIDECAR, verify internal handling at STATE-COLUMN-SIDECAR-RECEIVED. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: A DataColumnSidecar received from the P2P network must have its column index within the range [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS) before any further processing. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
executable_checks: Verify correct state transition at STATE-COLUMN-SIDECAR-RECEIVED after boundary crossing
Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between validation and use
Confirm no data corruption in internal processing path
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-PRECOND-001. Supporting boundary check. Nodes under test: ['STATE-COLUMN-SIDECAR-RECEIVED', 'ACTION-VALIDATE-SIDECAR-FORMAT'].

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at ACTION-VALIDATE-DATA

reachability.notes: Provider sends invalid cell data that bypasses KZG validation due to implementation bug.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-PROVIDER-VALIDATE, verify internal handling at ACTION-VALIDATE-DATA. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: Cell data received from providers must be validated per EIP-7594 specifications (KZG proofs) before being accepted and propagated. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
executable_checks: Verify correct state transition at ACTION-VALIDATE-DATA after boundary crossing
Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between validation and use
Confirm no data corruption in internal processing path
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP8070-PRECOND-006. Supporting boundary check. Nodes under test: ['ACTION-VALIDATE-DATA', 'STATE-DATA-VALIDATED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at ACTION-COMPUTE-MINIMUM-REQUEST-EPOCH

reachability.notes: Prevents requests for expired data

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-COMPUTE-MIN-EPOCH, verify internal handling at ACTION-COMPUTE-MINIMUM-REQUEST-EPOCH. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: Before serving a DataColumnSidecarsByRoot request, the node MUST compute minimum_request_epoch = max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH) and return ResourceUnavailable error code 3 for block roots with epoch < minimum_request_epoch. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
executable_checks: Verify correct state transition at ACTION-COMPUTE-MINIMUM-REQUEST-EPOCH after boundary crossing
Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between validation and use
Confirm no data corruption in internal processing path

checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: Attackers must not be able to manipulate execution gas prices to artificially inflate or deflate blob costs via the rese

reachability.notes: Attacker could submit transactions to manipulate base_fee_per_gas, indirectly affecting blob pricing via the reserve mechanism.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at ACTION-COMPUTE-RESERVE-PRICE: Attackers must not be able to manipulate execution gas prices to artificially inflate or deflate blob costs via the reserve price mechanism. The linkage between execution and blob fees must not create profitable manipulation strategies. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.

Test with boundary values (0, max, overflow)
Verify invariant holds across all transitions
Cross-client comparison if consensus-relevant

reachability.notes: False positive rejection of valid transactions would break normal operation.

Verify the postcondition: Transactions with gasLimit <= 16,777,216 MUST be accepted into the mempool (assuming other validation passes). 3. Check for unexpected side effects or state changes. 4. Test with edge-case inputs that stress the postcondition. 5. Cross-client comparison of output states.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at STATE-POST-MERGE-SYNC-ONLY

reachability.notes: Malicious peers sending blocks with pre-merge fields still present (or missing required post-merge fields) via eth/69 could exploit parsing bugs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at STATE-POST-MERGE-SYNC-ONLY, verify internal handling at STATE-POST-MERGE-SYNC-ONLY. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: eth/69 protocol implementations MUST correctly handle the absence of totalDifficulty and other pre-merge fields. Peers sending blocks with unexpected field presence/absence MUST be disconnected. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at ACTION-BYTES-TO-FIELD-ELEMENTS

reachability.notes: Values >= BLS_MODULUS in cell data could cause incorrect field arithmetic, potentially allowing forged proofs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-DESER-FIELD, verify internal handling at ACTION-BYTES-TO-FIELD-ELEMENTS. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: Each field element decoded via bytes_to_bls_field() MUST be validated to be < BLS_MODULUS, rejecting values that would wrap around. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
